programmes.show_name: Nature and Us: A History through Art
programmes.show_description: 2/3. James Fox takes us from East Asia and India to Europe and America, charting the 1,000-year story about our quest to understand nature.

The surprising tales behind treasures such as the Tara Brooch, the Broighter Hoard, the Waterford Charter Roll and others that have helped to shape and create modern Ireland.

programmes.show_name: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programmes.show_description: 3/7. The documentary series taking a comprehensive look at British history. Henry II built a great medieval empire - which was then torn apart by his scheming family.

programmes.show_name: Portrait of a Confused Father - Storyville
programmes.show_description: Deeply personal film charting a father-son relationship that takes a sudden, tragic turn. Contains very strong language.
Film following Irish ornithologist Seán Ronayne's mission to record the sound of every bird species in Ireland - that's nearly 200 birds.

programmes.show_name: Tracey Emin: Where Do You Draw the Line? - imagine
programmes.show_description: Artist Tracey Emin talks to Alan Yentob about her life. Contains some strong language.

programmes.show_name: Emin/Munch: Between the Clock and the Bed
programmes.show_description: A look at the affinities between the art of Edvard Munch and Tracey Emin. Contains some strong language.

Wildlife film-maker Martin Dohrn contemplates the end of his career as he turns his lens to the dramatic dance between a million seabirds and a single hunting falcon.

programmes.show_name: Britain's Wild Places: Dancing Cranes of Sweden
programmes.show_description: As the spring sunshine melts the snow and ice on a lake which is a stop-off point for 10,000 migrating cranes, the birds start to do an unforgettable dance.

programmes.show_name: Secrets of Size: Atoms to Supergalaxies
programmes.show_description: 1/2. In this mind-bending film, Jim Al-Khalili explores the universe at its smallest, from tiny insects to living cells and viruses, before finally arriving at the atom.
Daniela Nardini looks back on the 1990s BBC drama series This Life, which followed the misadventures of a group of flat-sharing friends pursing careers in law.
